notes: Abdul Rahim Khan-i-Khanan. Akbar was the third Mughal emperor, and he ruled over India from 1556 to 1605.

Todar Mal. Navratnas of Akbar : Abul Fazl - Prime Minister and History chief advisor. Faizi - Education Minister. Birbal - Foreign Minister. Raja Man Singh - Chief of Staff of Akbar’s Mughal Army. Tansen - Culture Minister and Singer. Abdul Rahim Khan-I-Khana - Defence Minister. Mullah Do Piyaza - Home Minister. Fakir Aziao-Din - Religious Minister.

Punjab. Akbar was crowned at Kalanaur on February 14, 1556, following the sudden death of Humayun, who died after falling from the stairs of the Dinpanah library. At just 13 years old, Akbar came to power under the guidance of his guardian, Bairam Khan. He later defeated Hemu in the Second Battle of Panipat, securing control over Delhi. Q.381. Who among the following was one of the Navratnas of Akbar? SSC CGL 11/09/2024 (1st Shift) (a) Purandar Das (b) Swami Haridas (c) Tansen (d) Amir Khusro Sol.381.(c) Tansen. Mian Tansen, originally named Ramtanu Pandey is a legendary figure in Hindustani Classical Music. He is known as the "Sangeet Samrat," or "Monarch of Hindustani Music.

Bairam Khan. A prominent Mughal noble and military commander who served as Akbar's regent and chief minister during the early years of his reign. Q.383. Which amongst the following statements is correct about Mughal emperor Akbar? SSC CHSL 08/07/2024 (1st shift) (a) He founded a new religion called ‘Din-e Ilahi’. (b) He fought and won the third battle of Panipat against the Hindu King Hemu (c) He was declared emperor in 1602. (d) He was highly educated and knew many languages. Sol.383.(a) He founded a new religion called ‘Din-e-Ilahi’. The theory of Din-e-Ilahi means belief in one God. The first initiated disciples of Din-i-Ilahi during emperor Akbar included Birbal, Prince Salim, and Abul-Fazl ibn Mubarak. Q.384. Who amongst the following Mughal emperors got classical Hindu mythological texts Ramayana and Mahabharata translated into Persian ? SSC CHSL 09/07/2024 (4th shift) (a) Jahangir (b) Aurangzeb (c) Akbar (d) Shahjahan Sol.384.(c) Akbar was the 3rd Mughal emperor (1556 to 1605). The Mahabharata was translated by Faizi and `Abd al-Qadir Bada'uni and named Razmnama. Literature under the rule of Akbar: Akbarnama (Autobiography of Akbar) and Ain-i-Akbari written by Abu'l-Fazl. Q.385. Which of the following statements about Akbar are true? 1 He introduced the Mansabdari system. 2 He defeated and merged the state of Bijapur in his empire. 3 He made Fatehpur Sikri his capital. SSC CHSL 10/07/2024 (1st shift) (a) All 1, 2, 3 (b) Only 1 and 3 (c) Only 2 and 3 (d) Only 1 and 2 Sol.385.(b) Only and 3. The Mansabdari system was a system of ranking government officials and military generals in the Mughal Empire, which was introduced by Emperor Akbar in 1571. Aurangzeb, the sixth Mughal emperor (1658-1707), sent his armies to conquer the Deccan Sultanates. He annexed Bijapur in 1685 and Golconda in 1687. Q.386. When Akbar introduced Mansabdari system, _______ was the lowest grade of Mansabdar. Matriculation Level 20/06/2024 (Shift - 4) (a) fifty (b) one hundred (c) ten (d) one thousand Sol.386.(c) Ten. The term mansabdar refers to an individual who holds a mansab, meaning a position or rank. It was a grading system used by the Mughals to fix rank, salary and military responsibilities. During Akbar's reign, the lowest rank allotted was 10, and the highest was that of 10,000.

1601. Khandesh was situated in the Tapti valley, Maharashtra. It was founded by Malik Raja Faruqi. Major military campaigns by Akbar - In 1568 the Sisodiya capital of Chittor was seized and in 1569 Ranthambore. Q.388. Jaimal and Patta were the warriors who were given charge of defending the fort of ______ SSC CHSL 03/08/2023 (4th shift) (a) Raisen (b) Ajmer (c) Udaipur (d) Chittor Sol.388.(d) Chittor. Siege of Chittorgarh (1567 - 1568) - It was a military expedition of the Mughal Empire under Akbar against the Mewar kingdom that commenced in 1567 during which the Mughals successfully captured the fort of Chittorgarh.

Rewa. Tansen was initially the court singer of King Ram Chand. His original name was RamaTanu Pandey. Tansen was the title given to him by Raja Vikramjit of Gwalior. Q.390. During the reign of Akbar, _________ was the land that has lain fallow for three or four years. SSC CGL 21/07/2023 (1st shift) (a) Parauti (b) Polaj (c) Chachar (d) Banjar Sol.390.(c) Chachar. Parauti - The land kept out of cultivation temporarily in order to recoup its lost fertility. Polaj - The ideal and best type of land throughout the empire and it was cultivated always and was never allowed to lie fallow.

Fatehpur Sikri. It is located near Agra in Uttar Pradesh. It was built by the Mughal Emperor Akbar in the 16th century. Fatehpur Sikri was built during and 1585. Fatehpur Sikri was declared as UNESCO World Heritage Sites in 1986.

1575. The Battle of Tukaroi (Battle of Bajhaura/ Moghalmari) - Fought at Balasore (Odisha) in which Akbar (Mughal Emperor) defeated Daud Khan Karrani (Ruler of Bengal). The battle led to the Treaty of Katak in which Daud Khan Karrani ceded the whole of Bengal and Bihar, retaining only Odisha.
Sher Shah Suri's. Although the Suri dynasty ruled for only a short period i.e. 15 years, it introduced an administration that borrowed elements from Alauddin Khilji and made them more efficient.

Ancestors. Abul Fazl (Akbar’s court historian) wrote a three-volume history of Akbar’s reign, titled Akbar Nama. The first volume (Akbar’s ancestors), second volume (events of Akbar’s reign) and The third (the Ain-i Akbari). Ain-i Akbari is divided into five volumes . Q.395. Ajmer became the suba headquarters under the ______. SSC CGL 03/12/2022 (4th Shift) (a) Pallavas (b) Cholas (c) Mughals (d) Delhi Sultanate Sol.395.(c) Mughals. Ajmer Subah - One of the original 12 subahs of Mughal Empire (1526 - 1858). It was established during Akbar's administrative reforms of 1572–1580. The last Mughal - Bahadur Shah Zafar. Q.396. In which year was Kashmir annexed to the Mughal Empire by Akbar? Graduate Level 01/08/2022 (Shift - 4) (a) 1586 (b) 1590 (c) 1592 (d) 1580 Sol.396.(a) 1586. Military Conquests of Akbar (Mughal Emperor, 1556-1605): Malwa (conquered in 1562), Gondwana region of central India (1564), Gujarat (1573), Mewar (1576), both Bihar and Bengal (1576).
Akbar issued Mahzarnama in 1579. It is a declaration that gives authority to change the religious laws of Islam.

Akbar. Murshidabad was named by Nawab Murshid Qulī Khan. Akbar (reign - 1556 - 1605) - Constructed Fatehpur Sikri (made it his capital in 1569), Started a new religion Din-i Ilahi (1582), His biography titled Akbarnama (Wrote by Abul Fazl). Q.400. In the context of Mughal administration, which of the following is the most appropriate definition of ‘Abwab’? SSC CHSL 16/04/2021 (Evening) (a) A clerk in the government treasury (b) A court jester (c) A tax levied on the lands over and above the original rent (d) A reward for good governance Sol.400.(c) A tax levied on the lands over and above the original rent in Mughal India all temporary and circumstantial taxes and impositions levied by the government over and above regular taxes were called “abwab”. Jahangir Q.401. The Sisodiya ruler of Mewar, Amar Singh, accepted Mughal service during the reign of_________. Matriculation Level 25/06/2024 (Shift - 1) (a) Jahangir (b) Shah Jahan (c) Aurangzeb (d) Akbar Sol.401.(a) Jahangir. Amar singh was the eldest son and successor of Maharana Pratap. Akbar (1556–1605). Aurangzeb belonged to the aristocratic Timurid dynasty, and held administrative and military posts under his father Shah Jahan (1628 – 1658). Q.402. Who succeeded in securing trading concessions for the East India Company from Mughal Emperor Jahangir? SSC Stenographer 12/10/2023 (Evening) (a) John Mildenhall (b) Captain Hawkins (c) Captain Best (d) Thomas Roe Sol.402.(d) Thomas Roe was an English diplomat sent by the East India Company in 1615. John Mildenhall was the first British explorer to make an overland journey to India. Captain William Hawkins was an English sea captain who visited the court of Jahangir in 1608.

Mutamid Khan. He was in the court of Mughal Emperor Jahangir (4th Mughal ruler, 1605 to 1627). ‘Iqbalnama-i Jahangiri’ book was written in Persian and it covers the first 19 years of Jahangir's reign. Inayat Khan wrote” Shajahanama”. Abdul Rahim Khan translated “Babarnama” to the Persian language.

Punjab (Jalandhar). Shalimar Bagh, the Begum Shahi Mosque, and the Bachcha Taj are the monuments built by the Jahangir (Fourth Mughal emperor). Q.405. Mehrunnisa married Emperor Jahangir in ______. SSC MTS 26/07/2022 (Morning) (a) 1620 (b) 1602 (c) 1611 (d) 1603 Sol.405.(c) In 1611, Jahangir married Mehrunnisa who was known as Nur Jahan (Light of World). She encouraged Persian art and culture in the court.
